\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning**: Multiple studies highlight that surgical approaches to the TMJ vary widely depending on the condition being treated, with newer methods like minimally invasive or endoscopic techniques offering improved access and reduced complications—especially in preserving surrounding neurovasculature and enhancing visibility through tools such as endoscopy or navigational systems. While traditional approaches carry higher risks of vascular injury or poor visualization, modern innovations demonstrate clear benefits in targeted access and functionality preservation when applied appropriately.
